11 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Designing for Accessibility in 2026Web accessibility stopped being a nice-to-have in 2026. It is now a legal requirement, a revenue opportunity, and a real business risk if you ignore it, and the data behind that shift is impossible to argue with. In 2025, more than 3,100 website accessibility lawsuits were filed in US federal courts alone, up 27% in a single year. And here is the part that surprises most business owners: 77% of those cases targeted small and mid-sized businesses under $20 million in revenue, not just the enterprises.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Why Is It a Business Requirement Now?If you thought accessibility was a problem only for big companies or government sites, that assumption is now the expensive one. The uncomfortable truth is simpler: an inaccessible website is a lawsuit waiting to happen and a segment of customers you are quietly turning away.
This guide covers what changed, the laws you actually need to know (ADA and the new European Accessibility Act), the genuine business case beyond compliance, and a practical implementation guide your team can act on, colour contrast, keyboard navigation, screen readers, and alt text. One note upfront: this is practical guidance…
